questions.solution: Modulus \(=\sqrt{3+1}=2\). Argument \(=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)=\frac{\pi}{6}\). So the form is \(2(\cos\frac{\pi}{6}+i\sin\frac{\pi}{6})\). Common mistake: confusing \(\pi/6\) with \(\pi/3\) when computing \(\tan^{-1}(1/\sqrt{3})\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: If \(x^{2}+x+1=0\), then what is the value of \(x^{199}+x^{200}+x^{201}\)?
questions.solution: Roots of \(x^{2}+x+1=0\) are \(\omega,\omega^{2}\). Factor: \(x^{199}+x^{200}+x^{201}=x^{199}(1+x+x^{2})=x^{199}\cdot 0=0\). Common mistake: computing powers individually rather than factoring out \(x^{199}\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is the modulus of \(z=\dfrac{1+i\sin\theta}{1-i\sin\theta}\) where \(i=\sqrt{-1}\)?
questions.solution: Use \(|z|=\frac{|1+i\sin\theta|}{|1-i\sin\theta|}=\frac{\sqrt{1+\sin^{2}\theta}}{\sqrt{1+\sin^{2}\theta}}=1\). Common mistake: computing numerator and denominator moduli separately but then forgetting they are equal.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is \((1+i)^{4}+(1-i)^{4}\) equal to, where \(i=\sqrt{-1}\)?
questions.solution: Use \((1+i)^{2}=2i\) and \((1-i)^{2}=-2i\). Then \((1+i)^{4}=(2i)^{2}=-4\) and \((1-i)^{4}=(-2i)^{2}=-4\). Sum \(=-4+(-4)=-8\). Common mistake: computing \((1+i)^{4}\) by expanding all four terms instead of squaring twice.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is the value of \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{20}(i^{n-1}+i^{n}+i^{n+1})\) where \(i=\sqrt{-1}\)?
questions.solution: Each term \(i^{n-1}+i^{n}+i^{n+1}=i^{n-1}(1+i+i^{2})=i^{n-1}(1+i-1)=i^{n}\). So the sum \(=\sum_{n=1}^{20}i^{n}=i\cdot\frac{1-i^{20}}{1-i}=i\cdot\frac{1-1}{1-i}=0\). Common mistake: expanding each group separately instead of factoring.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.subtopic: Determinant with Cube Roots of Unity
questions.question: If \(\omega=-\dfrac{1}{2}+i\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\), then what is the value of \(\begin{vmatrix}1+\omega & 1+\omega^{2} & \omega+\omega^{2}\\1 & \omega & \omega^{2}\\\omega & \omega^{2} & 1\end{vmatrix}\)?
questions.solution: Since \(\omega\) is a primitive cube root of unity, \(1+\omega=-\omega^{2}\), \(1+\omega^{2}=-\omega\), and \(\omega+\omega^{2}=-1\). Substituting and computing, two rows become proportional, making the determinant \(0\). Common mistake: not using the identity \(1+\omega+\omega^{2}=0\) to simplify entries before evaluating.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is the value of \(\sqrt{12+5i}+\sqrt{12-5i}\), where \(i=\sqrt{-1}\)?
questions.solution: Let \(\sqrt{12+5i}=a+bi\); then \(a^{2}-b^{2}=12\) and \(2ab=5\). Solving: \(a=\frac{5}{2}, b=1\) doesn't work cleanly; using modulus: \(|\sqrt{12+5i}|=\sqrt{13}\). The two conjugate square roots add to \(2\text{Re}(\sqrt{12+5i})\). Setting \((a+bi)^{2}=12+5i\) gives \(a=\frac{5}{\sqrt{2}}\), so sum \(=2a=5\sqrt{2}\). Common mistake: trying to add moduli rather than finding the real part of the square root.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: \((1-\sin A+\cos A)^{2}\) is equal to
questions.solution: Expand \((1-\sin A+\cos A)^{2}=1+\sin^{2}A+\cos^{2}A-2\sin A+2\cos A-2\sin A\cos A = 2+2\cos A-2\sin A-2\sin A\cos A = 2(1-\sin A)(1+\cos A)\). Common mistake: sign errors during expansion of the cross terms.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is \(\dfrac{\cos\theta}{1-\tan\theta}+\dfrac{\sin\theta}{1-\cot\theta}\) equal to?
questions.solution: Rewrite with common denominator: \(\frac{\cos^{2}\theta}{\cos\theta-\sin\theta}+\frac{\sin^{2}\theta}{\sin\theta-\cos\theta}=\frac{\cos^{2}\theta-\sin^{2}\theta}{\cos\theta-\sin\theta}=\cos\theta+\sin\theta\). Common mistake: not converting \(\cot\theta\) to \(\frac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta}\) before combining fractions.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: The value of \(\sin^{2}5^{\circ}+\sin^{2}10^{\circ}+\sin^{2}15^{\circ}+\sin^{2}20^{\circ}+\cdots+\sin^{2}90^{\circ}\) is
questions.solution: Pair \(\sin^{2}\theta+\sin^{2}(90^{\circ}-\theta)=1\). The series has 18 terms (5°, 10°, …, 90°). Pair up: (5°,85°),(10°,80°),…,(40°,50°) — 8 pairs each summing to 1 — plus \(\sin^{2}45^{\circ}=\frac{1}{2}\) and \(\sin^{2}90^{\circ}=1\). Total \(=8+\frac{1}{2}+1=\frac{19}{2}\). Common mistake: miscounting terms or forgetting the unpaired middle terms.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: On simplifying \(\dfrac{\sin 3A+\sin^{3}A}{\sin A}+\dfrac{\cos 3A-\cos^{3}A}{\cos A}\), we get
questions.solution: Use \(\sin 3A=3\sin A-4\sin^{3}A\) and \(\cos 3A=4\cos^{3}A-3\cos A\). Then \(\frac{(3\sin A-4\sin^{3}A)+\sin^{3}A}{\sin A}+\frac{(4\cos^{3}A-3\cos A)-\cos^{3}A}{\cos A}=(3-3\sin^{2}A)+(3\cos^{2}A-3)=3-3+3=3\). Common mistake: not applying the correct triple angle identity for both sine and cosine.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: The expression \(\dfrac{\sin\alpha+\sin\beta}{\cos\alpha+\cos\beta}\) is equal to
questions.solution: Apply sum-to-product: numerator \(=2\sin\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}\cos\frac{\alpha-\beta}{2}\), denominator \(=2\cos\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}\cos\frac{\alpha-\beta}{2}\). Dividing gives \(\tan\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}\). Common mistake: confusing sum-to-product with product-to-sum formulas.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: If \(\tan(\alpha+\beta)=2\) and \(\tan(\alpha-\beta)=1\), then \(\tan(2\alpha)\) is equal to
questions.solution: Use \(\tan 2\alpha=\tan[(\alpha+\beta)+(\alpha-\beta)]=\frac{\tan(\alpha+\beta)+\tan(\alpha-\beta)}{1-\tan(\alpha+\beta)\tan(\alpha-\beta)}=\frac{2+1}{1-2\cdot1}=\frac{3}{-1}=-3\). Common mistake: using subtraction formula instead of addition formula for \(\tan(A+B)\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is \(\dfrac{\sin 5x-\sin 3x}{\cos 5x+\cos 3x}\) equal to?
questions.solution: Numerator \(=2\cos 4x\sin x\), denominator \(=2\cos 4x\cos x\). The ratio is \(\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}=\tan x\). Common mistake: applying wrong sum-to-product formula for \(\cos 5x+\cos 3x\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is \(\sin 105^{\circ}+\cos 105^{\circ}\) equal to?
questions.solution: Note \(\cos 105^{\circ}=\cos(90^{\circ}+15^{\circ})=-\sin 15^{\circ}\). So \(\sin 105^{\circ}+\cos 105^{\circ}=\sin 105^{\circ}-\sin 15^{\circ}=2\cos 60^{\circ}\sin 45^{\circ}=2\cdot\frac{1}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\). Common mistake: not converting \(\cos 105^{\circ}\) correctly using co-function identity.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: If \(\dfrac{\sin(x+y)}{\sin(x-y)}=\dfrac{a+b}{a-b}\), then what is \(\dfrac{\tan x}{\tan y}\) equal to?
questions.solution: Apply componendo-dividendo: \(\frac{\sin(x+y)+\sin(x-y)}{\sin(x+y)-\sin(x-y)}=\frac{2a}{2b}\). LHS simplifies to \(\frac{2\sin x\cos y}{2\cos x\sin y}=\frac{\tan x}{\tan y}\). So \(\frac{\tan x}{\tan y}=\frac{a}{b}\). Common mistake: applying componendo-dividendo incorrectly or not using sum-to-product on the left side.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is \(\dfrac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^{2}\theta}\) equal to?
questions.solution: This is the standard double angle identity: \(\sin 2\theta=\frac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^{2}\theta}\). Common mistake: confusing with \(\cos 2\theta=\frac{1-\tan^{2}\theta}{1+\tan^{2}\theta}\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: If \(A+B+C=180^{\circ}\), then what is \(\sin 2A-\sin 2B-\sin 2C\) equal to?
questions.solution: Using sum-to-product and the condition \(A+B+C=\pi\): \(\sin 2A-\sin 2B-\sin 2C=-4\sin A\cos B\cos C\). This follows from repeated application of sum-to-product with \(C=\pi-A-B\). Common mistake: misremembering the sign pattern of the conditional identity.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: \(\tan 54^{\circ}\) can be expressed as
questions.solution: \(\tan 54^{\circ}=\tan(45^{\circ}+9^{\circ})=\frac{1+\tan 9^{\circ}}{1-\tan 9^{\circ}}=\frac{\cos 9^{\circ}+\sin 9^{\circ}}{\cos 9^{\circ}-\sin 9^{\circ}}\). Common mistake: using \(\tan(45^{\circ}-9^{\circ})\) instead of \(\tan(45^{\circ}+9^{\circ})\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is the value of \(\tan 75^{\circ}+\cot 75^{\circ}\)?
questions.solution: \(\tan 75^{\circ}=2+\sqrt{3}\), so \(\cot 75^{\circ}=2-\sqrt{3}\). Sum \(=(2+\sqrt{3})+(2-\sqrt{3})=4\). Alternatively, \(\tan\theta+\cot\theta=\frac{1}{\sin\theta\cos\theta}=\frac{2}{\sin 2\theta}=\frac{2}{\sin 150^{\circ}}=4\). Common mistake: computing \(\tan 75^{\circ}\) and \(\cot 75^{\circ}\) separately with errors instead of using the \(\frac{2}{\sin 2\theta}\) shortcut.

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: If \(\tan\theta=\dfrac{\cos 17^{\circ}-\sin 17^{\circ}}{\cos 17^{\circ}+\sin 17^{\circ}}\), then what is the value of \(\theta\)?
questions.solution: Divide numerator and denominator by \(\cos 17^{\circ}\): \(\tan\theta=\frac{1-\tan 17^{\circ}}{1+\tan 17^{\circ}}=\tan(45^{\circ}-17^{\circ})=\tan 28^{\circ}\). So \(\theta=28^{\circ}\). Common mistake: dividing by \(\sin 17^{\circ}\) instead of \(\cos 17^{\circ}\).

questions.subject: Maths
questions.question: What is the value of \(8\cos 10^{\circ}\cdot\cos 20^{\circ}\cdot\cos 40^{\circ}\)?
questions.solution: Use the identity \(\cos\theta\cos(60^{\circ}-\theta)\cos(60^{\circ}+\theta)=\frac{\sin 3\theta}{4\sin\theta}\) with \(\theta=20^{\circ}\): \(\cos 20^{\circ}\cos 40^{\circ}\cos 80^{\circ}=\frac{\sin 60^{\circ}}{4\sin 20^{\circ}}\). But we need \(\cos 10^{\circ}\cos 20^{\circ}\cos 40^{\circ}\); multiply by \(\sin 10^{\circ}\) and use repeated doubling: \(8\cos 10^{\circ}\cos 20^{\circ}\cos 40^{\circ}=\frac{\sin 80^{\circ}}{\sin 10^{\circ}}=\frac{\cos 10^{\circ}}{\sin 10^{\circ}}=\cot 10^{\circ}\). Common mistake: forgetting the \(\sin\theta\) factor when applying the triple product formula.
